title: Calculating Optical Density Using MCID
author: Andrea Vogel
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.k6tczen

steps.instruction: If this is the first time, create a folder labeled “Your Name” on the desktop
Open MCiD using the icon on the desktop Stretch the program to cover both screens: the picture should be 1 whole screen
Settings --> Display Format --> Mono (12 bit) --> ok

Settings --> Input Selection --> QICAM – 12 Lightbox Camera --> ok Check that lightbox camera is on (blue and green lights) (switch is on top of camera) Check that color camera (on top of other scope) is also on, because they are daisy-chained together (switch is on top of camera)
film is on lightbox for this

Digitize button – looks like a video camera Focus using the camera use ink on film to determine if it is in focus If need to adjust magnification, use the crank that the camera arm is attached to has to be on digitize (dialog box open) in order to see it move click ok to take image
remove film for this step
Change pseudocolor (grayscale bar on top menu) to SPECT2VIS
Adjust light intensity using intensity turner on the light box until the blank field of view looks bluish-pink lumens ~1000
Ctrl+click on the Digitize icon --> Flat Field Correction
If there is already a flat field correction established, click “Clear”

Click “Acquire” to establish a new flat field correction Do not change settings!!! (but just in case, they should match the below): Correction source: Digitize Correction method: Pixel by pixel Frame average: 4 Smoothing and Median filters should be clicked Click “ok”
film is back on the lightbox for this part, looking at the standards slide
Study type (on left-side menu) --> Standards
Operations (on left-side menu) --> Calibration ROD – relative optical density

Calibrations (on top menu bar) --> Density Change to Cal Stds input lowest to highest values (zero first) Select a density standards file --> ok If you need to create a new calibration table, click “None” Enter numbers from data sheet into “Standards unit” column Data sheet nCi/mg x Decay rate = # entered Click cursor in “Value Read” column next to first standard value Digitize the standards Get a zero reading (from plain film), then procedure through the standards from lightest to darkest
Select curve type (on dialog box) cubic spline (smoothed) is usually used pick the one that fits the data best (by eye)
Extrapolate (under curve graph) will tell computer how to interpret data that falls outside the range of the standards “current curve type” for both min and max
Operations --> Sample
Have laptop nearby with Excel spreadsheet open (don’t use MCiD spreadsheet) example: %s %s
change pseudocolor to grayscale
Choose whether unilateral or bilateral measuring Usually use unilateral

Choose whether free-hand or use shape (rectangle or oval) For VP, use oval useful: ctrl + drag = changes size alt + drag = rotates ctrl + click shape on menu = get measurements (to keep consistent across days/accidental changes) F4 clears image, not data

Click on region of interest and background (2 clicks) to get data if you want, hit F4 to clear the image, then re-click to make sure you are getting close numbers Numbers in red are extrapolated from the standards
Input data into excel spreadsheet
Move to next slice and repeat process
When completely done with that animal, clear all the data using “Clear” on sheet
turn off camera on the microscope and cover the scope
turn off camera on the lightbox
put lens cover on the camera
wipe down the light box with sparkle and kimwipes
place blue lab bench sheet on the light box, soft side down
Shut down computer
get images
use micrometer to determine sizes
